items: What are you thankful for and what has been challenging for you since we last met?
Have you experienced God at work in your life lately?
Have you had the opportunity to care for your friends or neighbours this week?
How did you go with your application from the last time we met?
Read the passage aloud and then watch the video clip.
Have one person retell the passage in their own words with the group’s help.
Was there anything missed or added?

data.html:
1 Jesus entered Jericho and was passing through. 2 A man was there by the name of Zacchaeus; he was a chief tax collector and was wealthy. 3 He wanted to see who Jesus was, but because he was short he could not see over the crowd. 4 So he ran ahead and climbed a sycamore-fig tree to see him, since Jesus was coming that way.

5 When Jesus reached the spot, he looked up and said to him, “Zacchaeus, come down immediately. I must stay at your house today.” 6 So he came down at once and welcomed him gladly.

7 All the people saw this and began to mutter, “He has gone to be the guest of a sinner.”

8 But Zacchaeus stood up and said to the Lord, “Look, Lord! Here and now I give half of my possessions to the poor, and if I have cheated anybody out of anything, I will pay back four times the amount.”

9 Jesus said to him, “Today salvation has come to this house, because this man, too, is a son of Abraham. 10 For the Son of Man came to seek and to save the lost.”

items: What stands out for you in this passage?
What does this passage tell you about Jesus, God, people and you?
What does this passage say about acceptance?
If this passage is from God, what difference would it make in your life?
How are you going to apply this passage this week?
Who will you share this passage with?
